name: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd.

profile.description: HIVE Digital Technologies Ltd. operates green-energy-powered data centers engaging in Bitcoin mining and high-performance computing hosting. The company provides computational capacity to blockchain networks and is aggressively pivoting toward AI infrastructure, including a planned 320 MW AI Gigafactory in the Greater Toronto Area expected to house 100,000+ NVIDIA GPUs. Formerly HIVE Blockchain Technologies Ltd., the company rebranded in July 2023. Operations span Canada, Sweden, and Paraguay, with headquarters in San Antonio, Texas.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): HIVE executed a meaningful strategic pivot from pure Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC infrastructure, rebranding from HIVE Blockchain to HIVE Digital Technologies in July 2023 [1]. FY2026 revenue surged 158% to $297.8M, with BUZZ HPC revenue reaching $19.5M (+94% YoY). The company secured a landmark $220M, 3-year sovereign AI GPU contract with Bell Canada/Cohere for deployment of 2,304 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GB200 NVL72 GPUs [2]. AI Deployment (2/2): HIVE is actively deploying AI infrastructure with $35M contracted HPC ARR, procuring NVIDIA H200 and B200 GPU clusters, targeting $225M ARR by end of calendar 2026/early 2027 [2]. The Bell Canada deal represents specific, quantifiable AI revenue generation. Market Share (0/2): HIVE mines approximately 3% of global Bitcoin, but this is a commodity position with no pricing power. In the AI/HPC infrastructure space, the company is a small emerging player competing against hyperscalers and established data center operators [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): The planned 320 MW AI Gigafactory in Greater Toronto Area (25-acre site purchased for CAD $58M) is expected to house 100,000+ NVIDIA GPUs with total investment of ~CAD $3.5B and operations beginning H2 2027 [2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): Significant capital deployment into next-generation GPU infrastructure exceeds typical mining industry R&D spending [1].

inspiration.label: WEAK
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (1/4): HIVE's stated purpose -- 'provides infrastructure for emerging technologies; takes pride in exporting efficient, clean and renewably sourced computing power to customers across the world' -- is functional but generic [3]. It describes what the company does rather than articulating a deeper purpose or vision of a better future. Interchangeable with many green data center operators. Community Impact (0/2): No evidence of significant charitable programs, foundation, or community investment initiatives beyond standard corporate operations. The 29-person headcount limits community impact footprint [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): With only 29 full-time employees running a $297.8M revenue operation, the team is exceptionally lean. Glassdoor ratings of 4.2/5 with work-life balance at 4.9/5 and 84% recommendation rate suggest a positive culture, though the sample size is very small (16 reviews) [3]. Insider Buying (1/2): Net insider selling observed -- COO Luke Rossy sold 215,000 shares at $3.97 ($853K) and Director Susan Brock McGee sold 16,700 shares at $4.45 ($74K) in June 2026 [3]. COO Rossy has disposed of his direct holdings, a negative signal, though Executive Chairman Frank Holmes maintains significant ownership.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. FY2026 revenue surged 158% to $297.8M driven by Bitcoin mining ($278.3M, +164%) and BUZZ HPC ($19.5M, +94%) [1]. Debt-to-equity of 13.4% is excellent, well below the 70% target. Revenue growth YoY of 130.5% and 5-year CAGR of ~35% score at maximum, and Forward P/E of 17.75 is below the 20 target [4]. However, the company is currently unprofitable with -49.85% profit margin and -30.34% ROE, both scoring zero [1]. PEG ratio unavailable. Weighted sum of 5.0 on max weights of 8 (excluding unavailable PEG) yields infrastructure score of 6. Liquidity floor passes: avg daily volume 23.8M x $4.26 = $101.5M daily dollar volume [4].

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): HIVE's legacy Bitcoin mining business is a physical-world, energy-intensive operation that AI cannot substitute -- mining requires actual computational hardware, power infrastructure, and facility management [2]. However, Bitcoin mining itself faces margin compression from halving events and energy cost volatility, and the core mining business is not structurally protected by AI immunity in the way utilities or real estate are. The strategic pivot toward AI/HPC infrastructure positions HIVE as a potential AI enabler, but the company is still early in execution with $19.5M HPC revenue against $278.3M mining revenue [1]. Key risk: HIVE must compete against well-capitalized hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and established GPU cloud providers in AI infrastructure. AI Opportunity (1/1): HIVE generates quantifiable AI revenue through BUZZ HPC ($19.5M FY2026 revenue, $35M contracted ARR) and has secured the $220M Bell Canada/Cohere contract for NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GPU deployment [2].

infinite_game.summary: HIVE Digital Technologies operates with a finite mindset, chasing successive technology trends -- from blockchain hype to Bitcoin mining to the current AI infrastructure pivot. While the company shows strategic agility, its rapid pivots appear driven more by market opportunity than a deeply held Just Cause. The 29-person team and net insider selling suggest a lean, opportunistic operation rather than an organization built to endure across generations.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (1/3): HIVE's purpose centers on 'clean and renewably sourced computing power,' which is relevant but shallow [3]. The company has pivoted its core identity twice in under a decade -- from blockchain to Bitcoin mining to AI infrastructure. While the green energy commitment provides some continuity, the business model shifts suggest the company follows market opportunity rather than a durable purpose. Sinek would note that a Just Cause should survive business model changes, and HIVE's cause is more of a marketing wrapper than a strategic north star.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The decision to invest heavily in AI infrastructure -- including the CAD $58M land purchase for the 320 MW Gigafactory and procurement of expensive NVIDIA GPUs -- while still unprofitable represents a meaningful bet on the future [2]. Executive Chairman Frank Holmes has led the company through multiple market cycles since 2017. However, the pivot from Bitcoin to AI could be read as chasing the next trend rather than principled long-term investment.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): With only 29 employees, HIVE operates as a small, tight-knit team. Glassdoor ratings of 4.2/5 and 4.9/5 work-life balance suggest a positive internal environment [3]. However, the COO disposing of his direct shareholdings and recent insider selling undermine trust signals. The extremely lean headcount for a $1.14B market cap company raises questions about organizational depth and succession planning.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): HIVE has demonstrated meaningful strategic pivots -- from being the first publicly listed Bitcoin miner (2017) to rebranding as a digital technology company focused on AI/HPC infrastructure (2023) [2]. The current buildout of the 320 MW AI Gigafactory represents a substantial bet on transforming the business. However, the pivots appear reactive to market trends rather than driven by a deeper purpose, and the company has yet to prove it can execute profitably in the AI infrastructure space.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (0/1): HIVE's competitive language focuses on being 'first' (first publicly listed Bitcoin miner) and positioning against hyperscalers without acknowledging their overwhelming scale advantages [3]. No evidence of an infinite competitive mindset that uses rivals' strengths for self-improvement.

investment_thesis: HIVE Digital Technologies is executing an ambitious pivot from commodity Bitcoin mining to AI/HPC infrastructure, with FY2026 revenue surging 158% to $297.8M and BUZZ HPC revenue growing 94% to $19.5M [1]. The $220M Bell Canada/Cohere sovereign AI contract for NVIDIA Grace Blackwell GPU deployment validates demand, and contracted HPC ARR of $35M with a target of $225M by early 2027 provides revenue visibility [2]. The planned 320 MW AI Gigafactory in Greater Toronto Area could house 100,000+ NVIDIA GPUs, positioning HIVE as a meaningful North American AI infrastructure player. Low debt-to-equity of 13.4%, daily liquidity of $101.5M, and Forward P/E of 17.75 support the growth trajectory, with analysts at B. Riley ($8), Cantor ($7), and Rosenblatt ($5.50) all seeing significant upside from the current $4.26 [5].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on execution risk and current unprofitability. HIVE lost $0.66/share in FY2026 with -49.85% profit margins and -30.34% ROE despite 158% revenue growth -- the company is burning cash faster than it generates recurring revenue [1]. The CAD $3.5B Gigafactory investment is enormous relative to the $1.14B market cap, creating substantial dilution and financing risk [2]. Bitcoin mining revenue ($278.3M, 93% of total) remains subject to halving events, energy costs, and cryptocurrency volatility with a beta of 3.72. HIVE must compete against hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) with infinitely deeper pockets and established customer relationships in AI infrastructure. Net insider selling -- including COO Rossy disposing of direct holdings at $3.97/share -- raises questions about management conviction at current prices [3]. Only 29 employees manage a billion-dollar enterprise, creating key-person risk and organizational fragility.
key_catalysts: Bell Canada/Cohere sovereign AI GPU deployment going live in late 2026 to early 2027, expected to add ~$70M ARR and validate HIVE's AI infrastructure business model at scale
BUZZ HPC ARR scaling from $35M contracted to $225M target by early 2027, driven by NVIDIA H200 and B200 GPU cluster demand across enterprise and sovereign AI customers
320 MW AI Gigafactory in Greater Toronto Area beginning operations in H2 2027, potentially housing 100,000+ NVIDIA GPUs and establishing HIVE as a major North American AI data center operator
key_risks: Current unprofitability (-$0.66 trailing EPS, -49.85% profit margin) with massive capital requirements for the CAD $3.5B AI Gigafactory, creating dilution and financing risk that could impair shareholder value
Bitcoin mining revenue (93% of total) is subject to halving cycles, energy cost volatility, and cryptocurrency price swings -- a 50% BTC decline would eliminate the majority of current revenue
Competition from hyperscalers (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and established GPU cloud providers (CoreWeave, Lambda) who offer superior scale, reliability, and enterprise sales infrastructure
